content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Circumference of a Tree". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/gym/102694/problem/A
constraints: The input graph is a tree, i.e., it is connected and has no cycles.
content.inputFormat: The input will contain a single integer n, representing the number of nodes in the tree, followed by n-1 lines, each containing two integers u and v, representing an edge between nodes u and v in the tree.
content.outputFormat: The output should be a single integer representing the circumference of the tree.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: To find the circumference of a tree, we can use a depth-first search (DFS) to find the longest path in the tree. The longest path in a tree is the diameter of the tree, which is also the circumference.

code.Python3:
import sys
MAXN = 100005
graph = [[] for _ in range(MAXN)]
visited = [False] * MAXN
max_len = 0
def dfs(node, len):
    global max_len
    visited[node] = True
    if len > max_len: max_len = len
    for i in graph[node]:
        if not visited[i]: dfs(i, len + 1)
    visited[node] = False
n = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(n - 1):
    u, v = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
    graph[u].append(v)
    graph[v].append(u)
dfs(1, 0)
sys.stdout.write(str(max_len) + '\n')
